title: Soldier Gets in Trouble for Betting on Secret Raid
summary: A U.S. soldier helped plan a secret mission to catch the leader of Venezuela, President Nicolas Maduro. After the mission, the soldier made bets on a website called Polymarket and won over $400,000. The problem is that he used secret information to make those bets. That is called insider trading, and it is against the law. Now the soldier is in trouble and may have to go to court. People are worried that this kind of betting on important events needs stricter rules. The soldier’s arrest shows that using secret information to make money on prediction markets is not allowed.

background_read: The article talks about a soldier who used secret information to win money by betting on a website. This is called insider trading and it is illegal. The soldier was part of a plan to capture the leader of Venezuela. People are now worried that such betting markets need more rules to prevent this from happening again.
Article_Structure: WHO: A U.S. Special Forces soldier who helped plan a raid to capture Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro.
WHAT: The soldier was indicted for using classified information to place bets on Polymarket and won over $400,000.
WHERE: The soldier was stationed at Fort Bragg in North Carolina, and the bets were placed on the Polymarket platform.
WHY: The soldier used secret information to make money, which is insider trading and against the law.

perspectives.description: The soldier broke the law by using secret information for personal gain.
The case shows that prediction markets need stricter rules to prevent insider trading.
